content.text: Enterprise knowledge management with RAG turns a company's scattered internal documents — wikis, tickets, Slack messages, design docs — into a queryable, up-to-date knowledge base. The challenge is not retrieval accuracy alone; it is keeping the index synchronized with constantly changing sources, enforcing the same access controls that govern the originals, and giving users answers they can trust enough to act on without verifying every source manually.

attrs.alt: Enterprise RAG architecture: source systems (Confluence, Drive, Slack, Jira, GitHub) feed ingestion pipeline (extract, chunk, embed, metadata), stored in vector store with keyword index for hybrid retrieval. Query path: rewrite → permission filter → rerank → generate with citations. Freshness panel: event-driven webhooks, polling, deletion handling, staleness signals.

content.text: Ingestion: From Raw Documents to Searchable Chunks

Ingestion quality determines retrieval quality. A chunk that is too large dilutes the signal for similarity search; a chunk that is too small loses context. The goal is to split documents at natural semantic boundaries while keeping each chunk self-contained enough to be useful in isolation.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime

@dataclass
class Chunk:
    chunk_id: str
    doc_id: str
    source: str           # "confluence" | "drive" | "slack" etc.
    title: str
    text: str
    author: str
    updated_at: datetime
    url: str
    acl_groups: list[str]  # e.g. ["eng", "everyone"] — from source system
    page: int | None = None
    section: str | None = None

def make_chunk_id(doc_id: str, chunk_index: int) -> str:
    raw = f"{doc_id}::{chunk_index}"
    return hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

def semantic_split(
    text: str,
    max_tokens: int = 400,
    overlap_tokens: int = 50,
) -> list[str]:
    """
    Split on paragraph boundaries, respecting max_tokens.
    Falls back to sentence splitting when a paragraph is too long.
    """
    # Rough approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 chars
    max_chars = max_tokens * 4
    overlap_chars = overlap_tokens * 4

    paragraphs = [p.strip() for p in text.split("\n\n") if p.strip()]
    chunks: list[str] = []
    current = ""

    for para in paragraphs:
        if len(current) + len(para) + 2 <= max_chars:
            current = (current + "\n\n" + para).strip()
        else:
            if current:
                chunks.append(current)
            # If the paragraph itself is too long, split by sentence
            if len(para) > max_chars:
                sentences = para.replace(". ", ".\n").splitlines()
                sub = ""
                for sent in sentences:
                    if len(sub) + len(sent) <= max_chars:
                        sub = (sub + " " + sent).strip()
                    else:
                        if sub:
                            chunks.append(sub)
                        sub = sent
                if sub:
                    chunks.append(sub)
                current = ""
            else:
                current = para

    if current:
        chunks.append(current)

    # Add overlap: prepend last N chars of previous chunk
    result: list[str] = []
    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        if i > 0 and overlap_chars > 0:
            prefix = chunks[i - 1][-overlap_chars:]
            chunk = prefix + " " + chunk
        result.append(chunk)
    return result
Hybrid Retrieval: Dense + Keyword

Dense vector search finds semantically similar content but can miss exact product names, error codes, or jargon that differs from the training data. BM25 keyword search handles exact matches but misses paraphrases. Combining both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) captures the strengths of each:

from openai import OpenAI
from rank_bm25 import BM25Okapi  # pip install rank-bm25
import numpy as np

client = OpenAI()

def embed(text: str) -> list[float]:
    resp = client.embeddings.create(model="text-embedding-3-small", input=text)
    return resp.data[0].embedding

def reciprocal_rank_fusion(
    ranked_lists: list[list[str]],  # each list is chunk_ids in ranked order
    k: int = 60,
) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
    """Merge N ranked lists into one using RRF."""
    scores: dict[str, float] = {}
    for ranked in ranked_lists:
        for rank, chunk_id in enumerate(ranked):
            scores[chunk_id] = scores.get(chunk_id, 0.0) + 1.0 / (k + rank + 1)
    return sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

class HybridRetriever:
    def __init__(self, chunks: list[Chunk], embeddings: list[list[float]]):
        self.chunks = chunks
        self.embeddings = np.array(embeddings)
        self.id_to_idx = {c.chunk_id: i for i, c in enumerate(chunks)}
        # BM25 index
        tokenized = [c.text.lower().split() for c in chunks]
        self.bm25 = BM25Okapi(tokenized)

    def search(
        self,
        query: str,
        user_groups: list[str],
        top_k: int = 20,
    ) -> list[Chunk]:
        q_emb = np.array(embed(query))

        # Dense retrieval
        norms = np.linalg.norm(self.embeddings, axis=1, keepdims=True)
        sims = self.embeddings @ q_emb / (norms.squeeze() * np.linalg.norm(q_emb) + 1e-9)
        dense_ranked = [self.chunks[i].chunk_id for i in np.argsort(-sims)[:top_k * 2]]

        # BM25 retrieval
        scores = self.bm25.get_scores(query.lower().split())
        bm25_ranked = [self.chunks[i].chunk_id for i in np.argsort(-scores)[:top_k * 2]]

        # Fuse
        fused = reciprocal_rank_fusion([dense_ranked, bm25_ranked])

        # Permission filter — apply BEFORE returning anything to the caller
        allowed_groups = set(user_groups)
        result: list[Chunk] = []
        for chunk_id, _ in fused:
            if len(result) >= top_k:
                break
            chunk = self.chunks[self.id_to_idx[chunk_id]]
            if set(chunk.acl_groups).intersection(allowed_groups):
                result.append(chunk)
        return result
Query Rewriting for Better Recall
User queries in a knowledge management context are often short and ambiguous. Two rewriting techniques improve recall without requiring the user to rephrase their question:
HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embedding)

— ask the LLM to write a short hypothetical answer to the query, then embed that answer rather than the raw query. The embedding of a hypothetical answer is closer in vector space to real answers than the embedding of the question itself.
Step-back prompting

— ask the LLM to generalize the question before retrieval. "What's the on-call rotation for the payments team?" becomes "on-call rotation policies engineering", which retrieves the policy document rather than a specific message.
Generating Answers with Citations

In an enterprise context, answers without sources are not trustworthy enough to act on. Prompt the model to cite each claim inline, and then resolve the citations to real document URLs in post-processing:
Keeping the Index Fresh
A stale knowledge base is worse than no knowledge base — confident wrong answers destroy user trust faster than "I don't know." The update strategy depends on the source system:

import asyncio
import hashlib
from typing import Protocol

class VectorStore(Protocol):
    def upsert(self, chunk_id: str, embedding: list[float], metadata: dict) -> None: ...
    def delete(self, chunk_ids: list[str]) -> None: ...
    def list_chunk_ids_for_doc(self, doc_id: str) -> list[str]: ...

class KnowledgeIndexer:
    def __init__(self, store: VectorStore):
        self.store = store
        # doc_id -> content_hash (to detect unchanged docs)
        self._hash_cache: dict[str, str] = {}

    def _content_hash(self, text: str) -> str:
        return hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

    async def upsert_document(
        self,
        doc_id: str,
        title: str,
        text: str,
        metadata: dict,
    ) -> bool:
        """Returns True if the document was updated, False if unchanged."""
        h = self._content_hash(text)
        if self._hash_cache.get(doc_id) == h:
            return False  # unchanged — skip re-embedding

        # Remove old chunks
        old_chunk_ids = self.store.list_chunk_ids_for_doc(doc_id)
        if old_chunk_ids:
            self.store.delete(old_chunk_ids)

        # Create new chunks
        texts = semantic_split(text)
        for i, chunk_text in enumerate(texts):
            chunk_id = make_chunk_id(doc_id, i)
            embedding = embed(f"{title}\n{chunk_text}")
            self.store.upsert(
                chunk_id=chunk_id,
                embedding=embedding,
                metadata={**metadata, "doc_id": doc_id, "chunk_index": i,
                           "title": title, "text": chunk_text},
            )
        self._hash_cache[doc_id] = h
        return True

    async def delete_document(self, doc_id: str) -> None:
        chunk_ids = self.store.list_chunk_ids_for_doc(doc_id)
        if chunk_ids:
            self.store.delete(chunk_ids)
        self._hash_cache.pop(doc_id, None)
Webhook Handler for Event-Driven Updates

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, BackgroundTasks
import httpx

app = FastAPI()
indexer: KnowledgeIndexer = ...  # injected at startup

@app.post("/webhooks/confluence")
async def confluence_webhook(request: Request, bg: BackgroundTasks):
    payload = await request.json()
    event_type = payload.get("eventType", "")
    page = payload.get("page", {})

    if event_type in ("page_created", "page_updated"):
        bg.add_task(_reindex_confluence_page, page["id"], page)
    elif event_type == "page_deleted":
        bg.add_task(indexer.delete_document, f"confluence::{page['id']}")

    return {"status": "queued"}

async def _reindex_confluence_page(page_id: str, page: dict):
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as http:
        resp = await http.get(
            f"https://your-domain.atlassian.net/wiki/rest/api/content/{page_id}",
            params={"expand": "body.storage,metadata.labels,space"},
            auth=("email@company.com", CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN),
        )
    data = resp.json()
    html_body = data["body"]["storage"]["value"]
    # Convert Confluence storage XML to plain text
    import re
    text = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", " ", html_body)
    text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()

    await indexer.upsert_document(
        doc_id=f"confluence::{page_id}",
        title=data["title"],
        text=text,
        metadata={
            "source": "confluence",
            "url": f"https://your-domain.atlassian.net/wiki{data['_links']['webui']}",
            "space": data["space"]["key"],
            "updated_at": data["version"]["when"],
            "author": data["version"]["by"]["displayName"],
            "acl_groups": ["everyone"],  # replace with actual space permissions
        },
    )
Measuring Knowledge Base Quality
Three metrics reveal whether the knowledge base is actually useful, independent of user satisfaction scores:
Metric
How to measure
Target
Retrieval precision
Sample 50 queries with known ground-truth sources; measure fraction of top-5 results that are relevant
≥ 0.7 at top-5
Answer faithfulness
LLM-as-judge: does the answer contradict any retrieved chunk? (RAGAS faithfulness score)
No-answer rate
Fraction of queries where the model correctly says "I don't know" vs. hallucinating
Hallucination rate < 5%
Index freshness
Median age of chunks (updated_at vs. now); fraction older than 30 days
< 20% of chunks > 30 days old
